engineGuide.h2: Volkswagen CC Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volkswagen CC across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Most common CC diesel code; 140hp output dominated fleet and company car registrations across SE and GT trims.
commonFailures: EGR cooler leaks causing coolant loss (60k-90k miles)
DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles
Turbo actuator wear at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFFB Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output 170/177hp variant common in Sport, GT and BlueMotion trims; popular with performance-minded diesel buyers.
compatibleVariants: Sport, GT, R-Line, BlueMotion, Exclusive, 4Motion GT
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging reducing performance
Timing chain tensioner wear (pre-2013 units)
Injector seal leaks causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFGB Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated EA288-compliant code for post-2013 BlueMotion models; improved emissions hardware over earlier EA188 units.
commonFailures: AdBlue system faults on late models
EGR cooler recall-related failures
DPF regeneration issues in urban use
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFGC Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Final EA288 iteration with SCR/AdBlue; fitted to late-model Exclusive and high-spec variants for emissions compliance.
commonFailures: AdBlue injector crystallisation
NOx sensor failures triggering limp mode
Turbo wastegate sticking at high mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for CRLB Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Dominant performance petrol code; 200-210hp output powered GT, R-Line and Exclusive trims with strong aftermarket support.
compatibleVariants: GT, R-Line, Exclusive, 4Motion GT, DSG GT
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure pre-2013 (rattle at startup)
PCV valve failure causing oil consumption
Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCZB Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early EA888 code for pre-facelift CC models; widespread in 2008-2012 GT and Sport trims before updated tensioner fitment.
commonFailures: Original timing chain tensioner prone to failure (50k-80k miles)
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starts
Carbon deposits on intake valves affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCTA Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level TSI option for SE and mid-spec trims; 160hp output balanced efficiency with adequate performance for the CC's weight.
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch on pre-2011 units
Water pump failure causing overheating
Intake manifold flap motor failure (P2014 code)
entries.cta: Get quotes for CDAA Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Updated EA888 Gen 3 code for post-2014 models; improved reliability over earlier iterations with revised PCV and timing components.
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves (all direct injection)
Turbocharger oil seal leaks at high mileage
Thermostat housing plastic cracking
entries.cta: Get quotes for CUW Volkswagen CC engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Flagship VR6 powerplant for GT S and top-spec CC models; 300hp smooth V6 character with 4Motion standard on most applications.
compatibleVariants: GT S, 4Motion GT S, Exclusive VR6
commonFailures: Coolant flange plastic cracking causing leaks
Timing chain guide wear at very high mileage
Oil consumption from valve stem seals (100k+ miles)

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen CC Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure - Why Pre-2013 Volkswagen CC 2.0 TSI Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen CC GT, Sport, R-Line 2.0 TSI (2008-2012, CCTA/CCZB engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Original timing chain tensioner design could lose pressure, allowing chain stretch and potential valve damage if not addressed early.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides and chain before catastrophic failure; requires engine front cover removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain jump; prevents major engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete timing chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets and seals; engine may need partial strip
repairOptions.longevity: Extends engine life significantly if block/head undamaged; best for lower-mileage CCs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with updated tensioner components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; recommended if valve damage occurred or engine has 100k+ miles

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling at cold startup on a pre-2013 CC 2.0 TSI, act immediately - preventative tensioner replacement is cost-effective. If the chain has already jumped or the engine has suffered damage, a rebuilt unit with updated components offers better long-term value than major in-situ repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CCZB/CCTA engine prices for your Volkswagen CC

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Leak - Why Volkswagen CC 2.0 TDI Engines Lose Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen CC SE, Sport, GT, BlueMotion 2.0 TDI (2008-2017, CFFB/CFGB/CFGC engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Internal EGR cooler cracks allow exhaust gases into coolant passages, causing overheating, white smoke and potential head gasket damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler assembly, flush cooling system, refill with correct coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught early before head gasket compromise; does not address underlying design weakness

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler, valve, pipes and associated gaskets; full system diagnostic and reset
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity for high-mileage diesels; addresses multiple weak points in one service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with updated EGR components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating caused head warpage or if engine has 120k+ miles with other wear concerns

problems.recommendation: EGR cooler leaks on CC TDI engines are often covered by manufacturer recalls - check your VIN first. If out of warranty and the engine has overheated significantly, a rebuilt unit with updated components is more reliable than patch repairs on a high-mileage diesel.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CFFB/CFGB Volkswagen CC diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Water Pump Failure - Why Volkswagen CC 1.8/2.0 TSI Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen CC SE, Sport, GT 1.8/2.0 TSI (2008-2012, CDAA/CCTA engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Plastic impeller water pumps can crack or seize, causing coolant loss and rapid overheating that may warp cylinder heads if not addressed immediately.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump, thermostat, gasket and coolant; pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught before overheating damage; use metal-impeller aftermarket pump for longevity

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump, thermostat, hoses, radiator cap and flush system; inspect head for warpage
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for preventative maintenance on 80k+ mile CCs; reduces repeat failure risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if overheating caused head warpage or gasket failure; only option for severely damaged engines

problems.recommendation: Water pump failure is often a warning sign rather than a terminal fault. If your CC overheated but was stopped quickly, a cooling system overhaul with metal-impeller pump may suffice. If the engine ran hot for extended periods, compression testing is essential before deciding between repair and replacement.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CDAA/CCTA engine prices for your Volkswagen CC

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup - Why Direct Injection Volkswagen CC Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen CC 1.8/2.0 TSI all trims (2008-2017, CDAA/CCTA/CCZB/CUW engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection petrol engines lack fuel washing over intake valves, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate and restrict airflow, causing misfires, rough idle and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media to remove carbon deposits
repairOptions.longevity: Effective temporary fix; recommended every 60k miles for preventative maintenance on TSI engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean intake valves, injectors, throttle body and replace PCV valve; ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity for high-mileage CCs; addresses multiple carbon-related issues in one service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with cleaned ports, updated PCV system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if carbon caused severe valve damage or if engine has 120k+ miles with multiple wear concerns

problems.recommendation: Carbon buildup is a maintenance issue, not a design flaw. Regular walnut shell cleaning every 60k miles prevents most problems. Only consider engine replacement if severe deposits caused valve damage or if the CC has other high-mileage wear concerns making rebuild more economical than repeated cleaning.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CCZB/CUW Volkswagen CC petrol engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Why Volkswagen CC Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen CC all 2.0 TSI and 2.0 TDI variants (2008-2017, multiple engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil seals wear allowing oil into intake/exhaust, or wastegate actuators stick causing boost control faults; often secondary to poor maintenance or oil quality issues.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, clean vanes, reset adaptations; oil and filter change
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for boost control faults without mechanical turbo damage; quick fix for early symptoms

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit refurbished turbocharger with new gaskets, lines and oil service
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo is mechanically failed but engine block/head undamaged; cost-effective for lower-mileage CCs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if turbo failure caused oil contamination damage to engine internals or if CC has 120k+ miles

problems.recommendation: Turbo failure is often a symptom of underlying oil maintenance issues. If your CC has full service history and the turbo failed in isolation, replacement turbo may suffice. If oil changes were neglected or the engine shows signs of oil starvation, a rebuilt engine with updated turbo offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt turbo-equipped Volkswagen CC engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen CC
finalCta.paragraph: The Volkswagen CC's most common engine problems - timing chain tensioner wear on pre-2013 TSI units, EGR cooler leaks on TDI diesels, and carbon buildup on all direct injection engines - are well understood by UK specialists. EngineMarket connects you with trusted rebuilders who fit upgraded components addressing these known weaknesses, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from multiple UK specialists to find the best value supply-only or supply & fit solution for your CC, without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen CC Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen CC and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen CC Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volkswagen CC across UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement unit.

items.title: Volkswagen CC 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TDI diesel powered the majority of UK Volkswagen CC models from launch to end of production, offering strong torque and fuel economy ideal for the CC's grand tourer character. Available in 140hp, 170hp and 177hp outputs across SE, Sport, GT, BlueMotion and Exclusive trims, this versatile engine family dominates replacement enquiries due to high fleet and company car usage.
commonFailurePoints: turbo actuator wear at high mileage

items.title: Volkswagen CC 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.4L TSI petrol offered modest performance and efficiency for early CC models, though limited UK availability means replacement enquiries are less common than larger displacements. Primarily fitted to base SE trims before 2012, this compact turbocharged unit shares technology with larger EA888 family engines.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner concerns on early units
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen CC 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen CC 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L TSI struck a balance between performance and efficiency for mid-spec CC trims, delivering adequate power for the CC's weight while maintaining reasonable fuel consumption. Common in SE and Sport variants before 2013, this engine shares the EA888 architecture with the more powerful 2.0L TSI.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch on pre-2011 units
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen CC 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen CC 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship petrol option for most CC trims, the 2.0L TSI delivered strong performance with 200-210hp outputs across GT, R-Line and Exclusive variants. As the most searched petrol engine for replacement, this EA888-family unit benefits from extensive aftermarket support and well-documented upgrade paths for known weaknesses.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen CC 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen CC 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The range-topping 3.6L VR6 powered the exclusive GT S and high-spec 4Motion variants, offering smooth V6 character and 300hp performance befitting the CC's premium positioning. Specialist replacement due to lower production numbers, this engine commands premium pricing but benefits from robust mechanical design when properly maintained. fuelTypes.h2: Volkswagen CC Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volkswagen CC engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from economical 2.0 TDI diesels to the flagship 3.6 VR6 petrol.

items.title: Volkswagen CC Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TDI diesel dominated UK CC sales across SE, Sport, GT and BlueMotion trims from 2008-2017, offering strong torque and economy ideal for high-mileage and company car use.
items.descriptor: The 2.0L TDI diesel dominated UK CC sales across SE, Sport, GT and BlueMotion trims from 2008-2017, offering strong torque and economy ideal for high-mileage and company car use.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen CC Diesel Engine Families
families: CFFB - 140hp workhorse code (SE/GT), EGR cooler leak risk at 60k-90k miles
CFGB - 170/177hp performance diesel (Sport/R-Line), timing tensioner update relevance pre-2013
CFGC - 177hp EA288 BlueMotion code, AdBlue system compatibility on late models
CRLB - 150hp final EA288 iteration, SCR emissions hardware considerations
foundIn: B6 platform CC (2008-2012) - CFFB, CFGB, early EA188 emissions hardware
B7 platform CC facelift (2012-2017) - CFGB, CFGC, CRLB with updated EA288/SCR systems
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (50-60mpg real-world)
EGR cooler recall coverage on many 2009-2014 units
DPF regeneration challenges for predominantly short-journey usage
Strong torque delivery suited to CC's grand tourer character

items.title: Volkswagen CC Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol CC models spanned 1.4L entry-level to 3.6L VR6 flagship, with the 2.0L TSI dominating replacement enquiries due to high sales volume in GT and R-Line trims.
items.descriptor: Petrol CC models spanned 1.4L entry-level to 3.6L VR6 flagship, with the 2.0L TSI dominating replacement enquiries due to high sales volume in GT and R-Line trims.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen CC Petrol Engine Families
families: CCZB - 200/210hp dominant TSI code (GT/R-Line), timing chain tensioner update critical pre-2013
CCTA - early 200hp EA888 code, original tensioner design requires preventative attention
CDAA - 160hp 1.8L TSI for mid-spec trims, shares EA888 architecture with 2.0L units
CDLJ - 300hp VR6 flagship code, specialist handling recommended for replacement
foundIn: B6 platform CC (2008-2012) - CCTA, CDAA, early EA888 with original tensioner design
B7 platform CC facelift (2012-2017) - CCZB, CUW, updated EA888 Gen 3 with revised components
VR6 applications (2012-2017) - CDLJ, BWS, 3.6L narrow-angle V6 with 4Motion standard
knownFor: Strong performance credentials, especially 2.0L TSI and VR6 variants
Carbon buildup on intake valves requires periodic walnut shell cleaning
Pre-2013 timing chain tensioner failures well-documented and addressable
Direct injection efficiency balanced with adequate power for CC's weight

items.title: Volkswagen CC Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Volkswagen CC was not offered with hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all variants featured conventional petrol or diesel engines only.

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen CC Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen CC's UK production run (2008-2017), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: B6 Platform Launch: EA188 TDI & Early EA888 TSI
years.description: The original CC launched with EA188 2.0 TDI diesels and first-generation EA888 1.8/2.0 TSI petrols, establishing the engine lineup that would define the model's replacement market.
keyChanges: CC nameplate introduced in UK, replacing Passat CC branding
EA188 2.0 TDI (CFFB/CFGB) and EA888 1.8/2.0 TSI (CDAA/CCTA) engines fitted
Pre-facelift styling with original timing chain tensioner design on petrol units
DSG and 4Motion options available across core variants
knownFor: Strong initial sales to fleet and company car sectors
Pre-2013 TSI timing chain tensioner failure risk now well-documented
EGR cooler leaks on early TDI units often covered by recalls
Good parts availability due to high UK registration numbers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2011 Volkswagen CC replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen CC 2009 engine replacement • CFFB EGR cooler fix • CCTA timing chain rattle repair

years.preview: B7 Platform Facelift: Updated EA888 & EA288 Introduction
years.description: The 2012 facelift brought revised styling, updated EA888 Gen 2/3 petrol engines with improved tensioners, and the first EA288 diesel codes for enhanced emissions compliance.
keyChanges: B7 platform facelift with revised front/rear styling and interior updates
EA888 Gen 2/3 petrol engines (CCZB/CUW) with revised timing components
EA288 diesel codes (CFGC/CRLB) introduced for improved emissions hardware
BlueMotion technology expanded across diesel range
knownFor: Updated timing chain tensioners significantly reduced petrol engine failure rates
EA288 diesel codes improved emissions compliance but added AdBlue complexity on late models
VR6 GT S variant established as performance flagship with strong enthusiast following
Strong residual values for well-maintained facelift models support replacement investment
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Volkswagen CC replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen CC 2012 engine replacement • CCZB rebuilt engine cost • CFGC AdBlue system compatibility

years.preview: Final Production: EA288 Maturity & Model End
years.description: The final CC production years featured mature EA288 diesel technology with SCR/AdBlue systems and refined EA888 Gen 3 petrol units, representing the most reliable iteration of the model's powertrains.
keyChanges: EA288 diesel engines (CRLB) with full SCR/AdBlue emissions systems standard
EA888 Gen 3 petrol engines (CUW) with further reliability refinements
Final model year 2017 marked end of CC production globally
Strong demand for late-model replacement engines from owners extending vehicle life
knownFor: Most reliable engine iterations in CC history, benefiting from iterative improvements
AdBlue system maintenance becomes relevant factor for late-model diesel ownership
VR6 GT S commands premium replacement pricing due to specialist nature and low supply
Strong enthusiast community supports parts availability and technical knowledge sharing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2017 Volkswagen CC replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen CC 2016 engine replacement • CRLB AdBlue fault repair • CDLJ VR6 specialist rebuild

faq.h2: Volkswagen CC Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen CC engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Volkswagen CC engine prices range from £740 for a basic 2.0 TDI unit to £3,200 for a 3.6 VR6, depending on engine code, variant and supplier. Supply-only pricing excludes fitting labour.
keyPoints: 2.0 TDI diesel rebuilt: £740-£2,450 depending on code and spec
2.0 TSI petrol rebuilt: £890-£2,850 with tensioner updates included
3.6 VR6 specialist rebuild: £2,100-£3,200 due to complexity
Supply & fit labour typically adds £450-£850 nationwide
items.cta: Compare live Volkswagen CC engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit my Volkswagen CC?
items.answer: Common Volkswagen CC engine codes include CFFB/CFGB for 2.0 TDI diesel, CCZB/CCTA for 2.0 TSI petrol, and CDLJ for the 3.6 VR6. Verify your exact code via DVLA registration check before ordering.
keyPoints: Diesel: CFFB (140hp), CFGB (170/177hp), CFGC (177hp EA288), CRLB (150hp SCR)
Petrol: CCTA (early 200hp), CCZB (200/210hp updated), CUW (late 200hp Gen 3)
VR6: CDLJ (300hp flagship), BWS/CKMA (earlier VR6 applications)
Enter registration above for instant code verification and compatible quotes
items.cta: Verify your Volkswagen CC engine code and get instant quotes

items.question: Is a reconditioned Volkswagen CC engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes - reputable UK specialists rebuild Volkswagen CC engines to OEM specification with upgraded components addressing known weaknesses, backed by minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranties.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units include updated timing tensioners/EGR components where applicable
Bench or dyno testing verifies performance before dispatch
Minimum 12-month warranty covers parts and labour at approved fitters
Choose suppliers with Volkswagen CC-specific experience for best results
items.cta: Find trusted Volkswagen CC engine rebuilders with warranty coverage

items.question: How long does Volkswagen CC engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 3-5 days for engine supply plus 1-2 days for fitting at an approved garage, though urgent options may be available for critical failures.
keyPoints: Standard rebuilt engine dispatch: 3-5 working days from order confirmation
Specialist units (VR6) or custom specifications may require 7-10 days
Approved fitting centres can typically complete installation in 1-2 days
Supply & fit packages coordinate both stages for single-point responsibility
items.cta: Get urgent Volkswagen CC engine replacement quotes if needed

items.question: Do Volkswagen CC engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with optional extended coverage available at checkout.

Unlimited mileage coverage protects high-mileage CC owners
Warranty valid at approved fitting centres nationwide
Claim process supported by specialist technical team for peace of mind
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen CC engine warranty terms from multiple suppliers

items.question: Can I keep my DSG gearbox with a replacement Volkswagen CC engine?
items.answer: Yes - replacement Volkswagen CC engines are compatible with factory DSG transmissions; the mechatronic unit remains separate and is not included in engine supply pricing.
keyPoints: Engine and DSG are separate assemblies; compatibility is maintained across codes
Mechatronic unit, clutch pack and fluids not included in engine supply price
Specialist fitting centres can inspect/refresh DSG during engine replacement
Confirm transmission condition before committing to engine replacement investment
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen CC DSG-compatible engine replacement

items.question: What causes Volkswagen CC timing chain failure?
items.answer: Pre-2013 Volkswagen CC 2.0 TSI engines used an original tensioner design prone to losing pressure, allowing chain stretch and potential valve damage if rattling at startup is ignored.
keyPoints: Original tensioner (part number 06H 109 467) could fail 50k-90k miles
Rattling noise at cold startup is the key early warning symptom
Updated tensioner (06H 109 467 L) fitted to rebuilt engines addresses the weakness
Preventative replacement costs £650-£950 vs £1,100+ for damaged engine rebuild
items.cta: Compare rebuilt CCZB/CCTA engines with updated tensioner included

items.question: Is it worth replacing a high-mileage Volkswagen CC engine?
items.answer: If your CC has strong chassis condition, full service history and you plan 2+ years ownership, a rebuilt engine at £740-£3,200 often represents better value than vehicle replacement.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost (£740-£3,200) vs CC market value (£3k-£12k)
Rebuilt engine with warranty extends reliable life by 50k-100k miles typically
Factor in recent maintenance (brakes, suspension, tyres) when deciding
Company car/fleet owners may benefit from VAT-registered supplier invoicing
items.cta: Calculate whether your Volkswagen CC is worth an engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Volkswagen CC engine code?
items.answer: Your Volkswagen CC engine code appears on the engine bay sticker, timing cover, service book, V5C logbook, or can be instantly verified by entering your registration above.
keyPoints: Engine bay sticker (near bonnet latch) shows code like CFFB or CCZB
V5C logbook section V.5 may list engine code or displacement
Service invoices and MOT certificates often record engine details
DVLA registration check above provides instant, accurate code verification
